serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Veggie Delite 5-Piece Nuggets is approximately 100 g (5 pieces), typically consumed as a snack or side dish.

nutrient_source.title: Veggie Delite 5Pc Nuggets — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Veggie Delite 5Pc Nuggets (cr_id G00010427). Secondary nutrients estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (vegetable nugget / breaded vegetable patty) where HPB does not report detailed micronutrient breakdown.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on breaded vegetable product with low carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Veggie Delite 5-Piece Nuggets. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous breaded vegetable patty / vegetable nugget entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The very low carbohydrate content (2.36 g/100 g) means glycaemic load per 100 g serving is approximately 1.1, which is negligible. Estimation is conservative and clinically defensible.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 300 mg per 100 g approaches the borderline threshold; actual sodium may vary and packaging verification is recommended.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100 g) and high protein (10.7 g/100 g) moderating glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate content and high protein level result in a negligible glycaemic load, making this product generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-sugar dipping sauces and with attention to the breadcrumb coating contribution.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation with estimated trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) and borderline sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Although fat content is reported as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB source, deep-frying typically introduces trace trans fats and saturated fats from frying oil; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and prefer baked or steamed vegetable preparations to reduce AGE and trans fat exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and trace trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory AGEs and trace trans fats that can worsen joint inflammation; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product and opt for baked or steamed vegetable alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veggie Delite 5-Piece Nuggets are a processed vegetable snack product sold in Singapore, consisting of a mixed-vegetable filling encased in a seasoned breadcrumb coating and deep-fried to a crispy exterior. The product is marketed as a vegetarian-friendly option and is commonly consumed as a snack, side dish, or light meal component. Per 100 g, the product provides approximately 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat (as reported by HPB Singapore), and 2.36 g carbohydrate, making it notably low in both calories and carbohydrates relative to its protein content. The high protein-to-calorie ratio is unusual for a fried snack and may reflect a soy or legume-based vegetable filling. The deep-fried preparation method introduces trace trans fats and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) despite the low reported fat content, which is clinically relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular disease. Estimated sodium is approximately 300 mg/100 g, placing it in the borderline range for hypertension management. The glycaemic load is negligible due to the very low carbohydrate content, making it broadly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-sugar condiments. The product is not certified halal or kosher and contains gluten from the breadcrumb coating.
common_user_queries: Are veggie nuggets suitable for diabetics?
Are veggie nuggets good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macros are sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, cr_id G00010427, confidence 0.95). Secondary nutrients (sodium, potassium, dietary fibre, sugar, trans fat) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous breaded vegetable product entries, as HPB does not publish a full micronutrient panel for this item. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is medium due to reliance on estimation for secondary nutrients.
notes: fat_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g per HPB Singapore Foodo source (cr_id G00010427). This is confirmed zero per the HPB source; however, deep-frying typically introduces trace fats from frying oil. Trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g/100 g based on typical deep-fried vegetable product profiles from USDA FoodData Central analogues — this is not confirmed zero and is flagged as an estimation. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 as consistent with the HPB-reported fat_g of 0.0; confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product. calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient data not available in HPB Singapore Foodo for this product and no sufficiently close analogue in USDA FoodData Central provides a defensible estimate for all these fields. food_subgroup corrected from 'Leafy vegetables' to 'Processed vegetable products' as this is a deep-fried breaded nugget product, not a raw leafy vegetable. Halal status is not confirmed; the product may or may not be halal-certified — consumers should verify with the vendor.
